program_name: Checkout for X340, Setup energy ramp 7.1 to 7.7 keV
Instrument & K-mono checkout for LU57, 8.2 keV, 50 fs
details:
Beam Requirements
•  We have to attenuate, so we prefer more beam 
spectral, spatial, and intensity stability at the expense 
of pulse energy
•  Beam pointing instability is a problem so we will overfill 
our lenses, improved stability in the past
•  8.2 keV, k-mono need maximum spectral stability (for 
intensity stability)

program_name: P141 POC:A. Batyuk X-Rays: 9.5 keV, 30 fs, SASE
details:
Questionnaire

Photon energy:

1. 9.5 keV

Comment: "between 8000 - 8800 eV, Very important. We want to exploit the large anomalous signal of Hg, Eu, Gd and Pt derivatives"

Pulse durration: 30 fs
Pulse energy: maximum
Operating mode: SASE

For the DD time at CXI in November, the users would like to use 2 different photon energies of 7.15 and 9 keV and change the pulse duration for damage studies. 

 Most likely we are talking about using the slotted foil to do ~10 fs or maybe try 5 fs pulses along with 20 fs and 40 fs.

XTCAV data will be taken

program_name: Phase plate and aspheric focusing commissioning for Std. Config
P143, 9.5 keV, 40 fs, 30 Hz with pulse picker.
details:
POC: Franklin Fuller
The users are requesting SASE mode with 9.5 keV central energy, 40 fs pulse duration, and maximum pulse energy for those settings. Users plan to run at 30 Hz (detector limited); we will be using the pulse picker to do that. The settings are the same as for LU50 and similar to LU34.

https://pswww.slac.stanford.edu/questionnaire_slac/proposal_questionnaire/run21/L-10011/#xray

For photon energy, we will scan in steps of 100 eV between 9 keV and 10 keV during the two first shifts. Afterwards, we expect to have found an optimum energy and remain there.

Pulse duration will be the more time consuming for machine tuning.

 

Shift 1 (Thu 12/08 night)

Long pulse duration (around 50 fs)

                Very coarse scan in photon energy (9-10 keV) and pulse duration (30-100 fs) 

 

Shift 2 (Fri 12/09 night)

Slotted foils (shortest achievable pulse duration is necessary)

                Users want to allow for long machine setup and test the shortest pulse duration they can get to have a clear comparison with the longer duration in their study

                They want to make sure the physics they want to study does not require shorter pulse duration that would not be able to achieve, and move on from there

                At fixed shortest pulse duration, very coarse scan in photon energy (100 eV steps from 9 to 10 keV)

 

Shift 3 (Sat 12/10 night)

Intermediate pulse duration mode (5-15 fs)

                No photon energy scan, only pulse duration scan

 

Shift 4 (Sun 12/11 night)

From there, we should know if the ultra-short or the intermediate pulse duration is better

                Users would stick to one or another, and perform finer scans

 

Shift 5 (Mon 12/12 night)

From there, we should know if the ultra-short or the intermediate pulse duration is better

                Users would stick to one or another, and perform finer scans

note: 10.235 GeV, Half charge and Slotted foil
